options.content:
Pinger
Open Detailed Info

targets.query:
from(bucket: "warehouse")
  |> range(start: -10m)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping_metrics")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${Devices_1}")
  |> last()
  |> pivot(rowKey:["_time"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> group()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({
      _time: r._time,
      latency: r.ms,
      device_group: r.group,
      hostname: r.device,
      current_status: r.status,
      location: r.location
    }))
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "latency", "device_group", "hostname", "current_status", "location"])
  |> yield(name: "status")

import "date"

stopTime = date.truncate(t: now(), unit: 1m)
startTime = date.add(d: -11m, to: stopTime)

from(bucket: "warehouse")
  |> range(start: startTime, stop: stopTime)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._measurement == "ping_metrics")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r.device == "${Devices_1}")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r._field == "ms")
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1m, fn: last, createEmpty: true)
  |> fill(value: 0.0)
  // Drop ALL tags (device, host, etc.) so only _time and _value remain
  |> drop(columns: ["_measurement", "device", "host", "_field", "group", "ip", "location", "status", "_start", "_stop"]) 
  |> group() 
  |> tail(n: 10)
  |> yield(name: "history_data")

list.definition:
import "influxdata/influxdb/schema"

schema.tagValues(
  bucket: "warehouse",
  tag: "device",
  predicate: (r) =>
    r._measurement == "ping_metrics" and
    r.group == "1 Scanners"
)
list.name: Devices_1
